name: Webprocess Conformance Demo
summary: The runnable proof of the Self-Driven Project Artefact contract+runtime (#1070 contract, #1071 runtime) in a real browser — slice C of #1026. The application knows only the contract surface (Step/ProcessRecipe/ArtefactRef + the driving loop) while the OPEN meta-schema registries, default-recipe resolution, the dependency-graph frontier, and the tolerance→autonomy throttle are asserted live. Invariants checked: the default seam ships the L0–L5 ladder + webprocess/default recipe, assertStep/assertProcessRecipe enforce the contract at the seam, indexSteps catches a dangling edge, runnableSteps yields the frontier, isRunComplete needs a final step, effectiveCeiling throttles down (never grants) and floors at L0, recipe ceiling overrides apply before the throttle, and the autonomy ladder is open.
description:
Concept

The runnable proof of the Self-Driven Project Artefact contract + runtime (#1070 contract, #1071 runtime), slice C of #1026. The application knows only the contract surface — Step / ProcessRecipe / ArtefactRef plus the driving loop — never the meta-schema internals.

Verified contract

The runtime-conformance section asserts the live we:process/ runtime against its contract: the default seam ships the L0–L5 autonomy ladder + the webprocess/default recipe; assertStep / assertProcessRecipe enforce the shape at the seam; indexSteps catches a dangling dependency edge; runnableSteps yields only the dependency frontier; isRunComplete needs a completed final step; effectiveCeiling throttles the autonomy ceiling DOWN per low-tolerance dimension (never grants, floored at L0), with recipe ceiling overrides applied before the throttle; and the autonomy ladder is OPEN to new rungs.

Dogfooded, not the spec

WE's own backlog is one dogfooded recipe — a consumer of the standard. The node ships the default; concrete recipes and the Plateau configurator are impl on top.
